mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary policy tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects constrained output and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60% of declining INDPRO observations during tightening periods, or predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal expanding residential investment and construction sector activity, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 58% of rising HOUST periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.015 over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces central banks to restrict liquidity, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance at p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment confirms broad-based labor market strength, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion indicates fading economic momentum and reduced corporate profit growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly returns occur in more than 65% of GDPC1 deceleration periods, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in walk-forward validation.
